title: mikania barahonensis
intro: Mikania barahonensis, also known as "Pandaka" or "Stemmadenia barahonensis," is a plant species native to the Dominican Republic and Haiti, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Mikania barahonensis include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of tea, infusion, or decoction, and it can also be applied topically to wounds and skin irritations.
